title: Pabstiella fluminensis
taxonomy: Pabstiella fluminensis belongs to the family Pleurothallidinae, which is a subfamily of orchids. It is a species of orchid, classified in the genus Pabstiella. This genus is part of the large orchid family, specifically within the subfamily Epidendroideae. Pabstiella fluminensis is native to Brazil and is a terrestrial orchid, meaning it grows on land rather than in water or on other plants. The classification of Pabstiella fluminensis is further divided into its species and subspecies, with the exact classification depending on the source.

intro: Pabstiella fluminensis, also known as Brazilian Pabstiella, is a species of orchid native to the tropical regions of Brazil. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, offering potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities, which can be beneficial in treating various ailments including wounds and respiratory infections. The bioactive constituents of Pabstiella fluminensis include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Pabstiella fluminensis include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to support immune function and alleviate symptoms of various health conditions.
